title: senna artemisioides subsp. oligophylla
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. oligophylla, also known as bush senna or Australian bloodwood, is a flowering plant native to Australia. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Senna artemisioides subsp. oligophylla include relief from constipation and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and used in herbal capsules or tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential side effects.
